trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Canada exports canola, wheat, copper, and aircraft to Turchia. Turchia exports vehicles, textiles, chemicals, and processed foods to Canada. Turchia is an important emerging market for Canadian exports.
disputes: Canada suspended drone component exports to Turchia after Azerbaigian-Armenia conflict (2020)

diplomatic.summary: NATO allies with a complex relationship. Canada has been critical of Turchia's democratic backsliding, its purchase of Russian S-400 systems, and its role in the Azerbaigian-Armenia conflict. Turchia and Canada have periodic friction over Kurdish issues and arms exports. Both remain aligned in NATO framework.
recent_incidents: Canada suspended drone exports to Turchia after use in Nagorno-Karabakh (2020)
Turchia NATO posture complicates Canada-Turchia defense ties

military.summary: Both are NATO allies but with significant friction. Canada supplies components used in Turkish Bayraktar drones. Canada suspended exports after the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict. Turchia's S-400 purchase from Russia remains a point of tension.
incidents: Canada suspended drone component exports to Turkey (2020-2022)

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Canadian mining companies have Turkish operations. Turkish construction companies have some Canadian market presence.

energy.summary: Turchia is interested in Canadian LNG as part of energy diversification. Limited current energy trade. Canada exports some agricultural feedstocks used in Turkish energy sector.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some Turkish students study in Canada. Joint research in engineering, computer science, and materials science.
